integrationKeys.createDescription: Issue a site-level bearer token for the Moodle / Canvas plugin. The plugin uses it to provision per-course API keys on behalf of teachers -- it cannot access course data itself. One key per LMS instance is usually enough.

callout.title: Key minted for {{name}}
callout.note: Copy this now; Minerva only stores the hash. Paste it into the Moodle plugin's site settings.

integrationKeys.listDescription: Revoking a key invalidates it immediately. Any api_keys the plugin already provisioned via this site key continue to work (they're ordinary course keys).
integrationKeys.empty: No site integration keys minted yet.

ltiPlatforms.setupDescription: Copy these values into Moodle's Site administration → Plugins → Activity modules → External tool → Manage tools, or the equivalent Canvas Developer Key. Any course in the LMS can then use this tool without per-course setup.
ltiPlatforms.siteLevelNote: On first launch from a Moodle course, the launching teacher picks which Minerva course to link to. That choice is saved as a binding and reused for every subsequent launch from the same Moodle course.
ltiPlatforms.listDescription: Each entry corresponds to one Moodle/Canvas instance. Removing a platform cascades to its course bindings; teachers will have to re-link on next launch.
ltiPlatforms.copyValuesHint: Paste the Platform ID (issuer), Client ID and Deployment ID that Moodle/Canvas shows after you save the tool config.
ltiPlatforms.deploymentIdPlaceholder: Leave blank to skip deployment ID check
ltiPlatforms.domainsHelp: Optional. If set, only launches whose claimed eppn ends with one of these domains are accepted. Leave empty to allow any eppn (the legacy behaviour).
ltiPlatforms.noBindings: No Moodle/Canvas courses have been linked to this platform yet.
ltiPlatforms.bindingsHint: Each row is a Moodle/Canvas course that has been linked to a Minerva course on first launch.

users.title: Users ({{total}})
users.description: Manage user roles, daily AI spending limits, and account status. Setting a role manually locks it from auto-promotion rules.

courses.title: Courses ({{total}})
courses.description: All courses on the platform. Click the settings link to open a course's configuration.

rules.title: Role Auto-Promotion Rules
rules.description: Each rule promotes a user to its target role when ALL conditions match the user's Shibboleth attributes at login. Rules never demote, never override admins (MINERVA_ADMINS env), and never touch users whose role was set manually in User Management (those users show a "locked" badge; click Unlock there to re-enable rule evaluation). Multi-valued attributes (affiliation, entitlement) use list-membership semantics for "contains"; substring matches are rejected.
rules.negatedNote: Negated operators (<0>not_contains</0>, <1>not_regex</1>) also require the attribute to be present on the user. This prevents external/non-Shib users (who have no affiliation / entitlement / etc. at all) from matching a "not contains X" rule by virtue of having nothing.
rules.empty: No rules yet. Create one above to start auto-promoting users.

card.noConditions: Add at least one condition for this rule to ever match.

externalInvites.createDescription: Generate a time-limited login link for someone without a Stockholm University account. Links bypass Shibboleth and grant student-level access; promote to teacher in User Management after they first log in. The full link is shown only once; if lost, revoke and re-mint.

callout.title: Invite link for {{eppn}}
callout.note: Share this link privately. It is shown only once and grants login until

storage.title: Storage
storage.descriptionTail: ; the host volume holds postgres, qdrant, documents and backups. Contact DSV helpdesk to expand when free space runs low.
storage.nearlyFull: Disk is nearly full. Request expansion from helpdesk soon.
storage.fillingUp: Disk is filling up. Consider requesting expansion.
storage.statsUnavailable: Disk stats unavailable on this platform.
